comparisonText: Berlin gets 9 hours 11 minutes more daylight in June than December.

january.label: JAN 1
january.comparisonText: After the winter solstice the days slowly lengthen again, gaining nearly an hour of evening light across January.

february.label: FEB 1
february.comparisonText: February adds daylight quickly, with sunset moving from around 5 pm to nearly 5:45 pm by the end of the month.

march.label: MAR 1
march.comparisonText: March transforms the evenings: the clocks go forward on March 28, 2027, pushing sunset close to 8 pm.

april.label: APR 1
april.comparisonText: April keeps stretching the evenings, with sunset reaching nearly 8:45 pm by the end of the month.

may.label: MAY 1
may.comparisonText: By May, Berlin enjoys long days and late sunsets, close to 9:25 pm by the end of the month.

july.label: JULY 1
july.comparisonText: Berlin still gets just over 9 hours more daylight on July 1 than around the winter solstice.

august.label: AUG 1
august.comparisonText: Berlin still gets almost 8 hours more daylight on August 1 than around the winter solstice, but the month loses light quickly.

september.label: SEP 1
september.comparisonText: Berlin still gets about 6 hours more daylight on September 1 than around the winter solstice, but the month loses more than an hour of evening light.

october.label: OCT 1
october.comparisonText: Berlin loses light fast in October, and sunset jumps back an hour when the clocks change on October 25, 2026.

november.label: NOV 1
november.comparisonText: By November, Berlin is down to about nine and a half hours of daylight, and it keeps shrinking toward the winter solstice.
